top_signals.title: Pricey Stocks Charge Into CPI, Earnings and Fed, All in One Day
top_signals.summary: Equities traders seem unperturbed by the dizzying array of key events on the calendar Tuesday, creating a precarious setup with the stock market hovering near a record high.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: China Cuts Saudi Crude Orders as Hormuz Risks and Discounts Reshape Trade
top_signals.summary: Some Chinese refiners have not nominated term crude cargoes from Saudi Arabia for August, while others have not been allocated any term supply for next month, as weak demand in China, competition from other producers, and continued disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz have slashed Saudi supply in recent months. At least two refiners in China have not asked for any term cargo deliveries for August, and a few others have not received provisional allocation volumes, traders with direct knowledge of the supply arrangements told Bloomberg on Tuesday.…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Ed Miliband Seen as Least Appealing UK Chancellor: Markets Pulse
top_signals.summary: Andy Burnham is viewed as bearish for UK assets, with former Labour Party leader Ed Miliband seen as the least market-friendly pick for Chancellor, according to Bloomberg's latest Markets Pulse Survey. (Source: Bloomberg)
